title: The problem of inconsistent results for statements with the same semantics
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(a INT, b VARCHAR, c FLOAT, d DATE);
INSERT INTO t1(a, b, c, d) VALUES (1, 'test', 1.5, '2023-01-01');
INSERT INTO t1(a, b, c, d) VALUES (2, NULL, 2.5, '2023-01-02');
SELECT a, b, c FROM t1 WHERE b IS NULL UNION ALL SELECT a, b, c FROM t1 WHERE c > 1.0;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t1 WHERE (a IS NOT NULL) AND (b IS NOT NULL) GROUP BY d;
key_bug_features: Inconsistent results for semantically identical SQL statements
